spoken_briefing: Today in AI tools for specialty contractors and small general contractors, your weekly briefing for June 15th, 2026. Starting with bidding and estimating. STACK from STACK Construction Technologies is a cloud-based estimating platform for trade subcontractors providing AI-assisted takeoff, bid management, and preconstruction workflows. Pricing: $2,999/year for single-user Takeoff and Estimating. Independent testing by Robotics and Automation News (February 2026) found STACK delivered accuracy within 3% of baseline across complex commercial projects. Togal.AI from Togal.AI is an AI-driven floor plan takeoff platform claiming 98% accuracy on architectural plan sets. Pricing is available on request. Coastal Construction saved 14.5 hours per plan set, approximately $1M in annual cost savings. BidIntell from BidIntell is an AI pre-bid decision platform that scores incoming bid invitations on a 100-point BidIndex based on location fit, contract terms, GC relationship history, and trade match, helping subs decide which opportunities to pursue.. Pricing: $49/user/month. Reduces wasted estimating hours on low-probability bids; focused on the pre-bid decision layer before takeoff begins.. Beam AI from Attentive.ai is an AI-powered estimating and bid management platform for commercial specialty contractors. Pricing: Custom annual subscription based on project volume. Starting around $299/month per some sources.. 1,200+ contractors and suppliers. Downtobid from Downtobid is an AI-powered construction bid management platform that reads uploaded plans, automatically identifies trade scopes, indexes every drawing, and sends personalized bid invitations to verified local subcontractors from a database of 250,000+ commercial subs.. Pricing: $149/month for GCs. Personalized invites generate 2x more responses than generic portal emails; reduces bid package creation from hours to 15 minutes.. ConstructConnect Takeoff Boost from ConstructConnect is an AI-powered automated takeoff rebuilt on Google Cloud using the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with NVIDIA GPUs, relaunched April 22, 2026. Pricing is available on request. Cuts takeoff hours significantly. Drawer AI from Drawer AI is an AI-powered automated electrical takeoff and estimating platform purpose-built for electrical subcontractors and estimators. Pricing: Starter $150/user/month. Starr Electric Company: analyzed 2,600+ fixtures and 3,400+ power devices with 70% time savings. TaksoAi from TaksoAi is an AI takeoff platform for HVAC, mechanical, and plumbing specialty contractors. Pricing is available on request. Processes most mechanical plans in under 15 minutes; identifies 60+ pipe and HVAC fitting types with high accuracy.. XBuild from XBuild is an AI-native agentic estimating platform for specialty subcontractors. Pricing: Subscription-based with unlimited AI estimates and no per-job fees. Free trial available without credit card.. 15,000+ projects processed, $250M+ in project value, 40,000+ estimating hours saved as of January 2026 launch (company-reported).. Trimble LiveCount AI Automate Takeoff from Trimble is an AI-powered automated takeoff capability within Trimble's LiveCount platform (part of Accubid Classic and Accubid Anywhere) and Estimation MEP Pro, targeting electrical and MEP specialty subcontractors. Pricing: Included in existing Trimble Accubid subscriptions. 95% drawing scale auto-detection rate. Quotr.ai from Quotr is a all-in-one AI construction estimating, takeoff, and procurement platform from a Berkeley SkyDeck-backed startup (Batch 19). Pricing: Monthly platform plans with a 7-day free trial. Per-project analysis pricing also available.. Vanderbilt University case study published. Space AI from Space AI is an AI-powered construction bidding and project management platform founded in 2025. Pricing: Free tier (limited projects). 500+ construction teams reported. ConstructionBids.ai from ConstructionBids.ai is an AI-powered government and public construction bid aggregation platform that consolidates live bid opportunities from 2,000+ federal, state, and local procurement portals into a single AI-scored dashboard. Pricing: Starting at $49/month. Consolidates 2,000+ government procurement portals into a single AI-scored feed. Kreo from Kreo Software is an AI-assisted 2D takeoff and 3D BIM cost estimating platform that automatically detects elements, cross-references data across drawing sheets, and generates cost reports. Pricing: Starting at $35/user/month. Design revision cost updates in minutes vs. Fresco from Fresco is an AI takeoff and estimating platform purpose-built exclusively for Division 8 specialty contractors covering doors, frames, and hardware. Pricing is available on request. 3,000+ takeoffs completed for GCs and Division 8 specialty subs. Rudus from Rudus is an AI-powered takeoff and estimation platform built exclusively for concrete subcontractors. Pricing is available on request. 70% reduction in concrete takeoff time. Bidi Contracting from Bidi Contracting is an AI-powered bid management platform for general contractors that unifies AI plan takeoff, subcontractor solicitation, and bid leveling in one workflow. Pricing: Starting at ~$3,600/year for GC bid management. Reduces bid package creation and sub solicitation time significantly. BuildVision AI from BuildVision AI is an AI construction bidding platform purpose-built for specialty subcontractors that produces a complete winning bid — including AI takeoff, bill of quantities, and branded proposal — in under one hour per project. Pricing: Custom pricing. Enables sub teams to respond to 2-3x more bid invitations with the same staff. Moving on to submittal and RFI tracking. Trunk Tools from Trunk Tools is an AI-native construction platform providing TrunkText (natural language Q&A across drawings, specs, submittals, RFIs, and contracts via SMS or Microsoft Teams), TrunkSubmittal (automated comparison of submitted products against spec requirements), and TrunkRFI (auto-drafts responses by scanning full document sets). Pricing is available on request. Revenue grew 5x in 12 months prior to Series B. $40M Series B closed (total $70M in funding), led by Insight Partners with participation from Redpoint Ventures and Liberty Mutual Strategic Ventures. Procore AI (Helix / Agent Builder) from Procore Technologies: Procore's embedded AI platform, dramatically expanded through the January 2026 acquisition of Datagrid (vertical AI firm). Pricing: Included in Procore platform. Buro Happold on track to reduce construction administration by 50% using Procore AI agents. Civils.ai from Civils.ai is an AI platform that extracts data from drawings, specifications, and building codes to enable automated RFI generation, advanced search, and compliance workflow automation.. Pricing is available on request. Reduces manual review time; catches compliance issues early to reduce RFI back-and-forth cycles.. BuildSync from BuildSync is a purpose-built AI submittal review platform that compares submitted product data against project specifications with 95%+ accuracy. Pricing is available on request. 95%+ accuracy on technical compliance checking; cuts first-submission rejection rates from 35% to 5%; reduces review time 70-80%.. SubmittalLink from SubmittalLink is an AI-powered submittal and RFI management platform purpose-built for subcontractors with transparent volume-based pricing and unlimited users on all plans. Pricing is available on request. Unlimited users at no extra cost; AI automatically generates structured submittal logs from spec books; mobile access for field review; automated owner reporting.. Constructable from Constructable is an AI-native field and project management platform built for mid-market general contractors running $20M-$150M commercial projects. Pricing: $850/month (Field Management). Unlimited users at flat rate; onboards teams in under 21 days; RFIs pinned to exact drawing locations; AI Answer Engine surfaces answers instantly from past project data.. Nomic AI from Nomic is a AEC-specialized AI platform built on LLMs trained specifically on construction documentation including drawing sets up to 200 pages and specifications up to 800 pages. Pricing: $40/user/month with minimum 25-seat commitment. Named BuiltWorlds 2026 Top 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions. Now for field management and daily reporting. CompanyCam from CompanyCam is a field photo documentation and contractor operations platform. Pricing: From $99/month (3-user minimum). $2B valuation as of August 2025. eSUB from eSUB is a mobile-first field management platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. Pricing is available on request. AI daily report drafting reduced foreman paperwork from 45 minutes to 10 minutes per day. Buildots from Buildots is an AI construction progress tracking and workforce intelligence platform using 360-degree cameras worn on hardhats to automatically compare actual progress against the project schedule. Pricing is available on request. Projects report up to 50% reduction in delays (2-3 months saved). Raken from Raken (backed by Sverica Capital) is a all-in-one field management platform for construction subcontractors and GCs covering daily reporting, time tracking, scheduling, safety management, workforce management, and project management. Pricing: $15/user/month (Lite), $25/user/month (Pro). 4,500+ construction firms and 70,000+ users. BuildOps from BuildOps is an unified commercial contractor management platform for specialty trades covering both service operations and construction project management. Pricing is available on request. 1,500+ specialty contractors on platform. InspectMind AI from InspectMind AI is a Y Combinator-backed AI platform for construction field inspections and plan checking. Pricing: $100/user/month (Field Reports). Generates field reports in minutes vs. iFieldSmart from iFieldSmart Technologies is a construction management platform with reality capture and AI-powered field documentation. Pricing: Starting at $14.99/user/month. Single 360 walkthrough generates full site documentation and digital twin intelligence; reduces site visit time and documentation overhead.. Kahua from Kahua is an enterprise construction project management platform for large GCs, program managers, and public sector owners. Pricing is available on request. Named Top Construction Technology Firm in Construction Executive's 2025 Top Tech report. OpenSpace from OpenSpace is an AI-powered 360-degree site capture and visual intelligence platform. Pricing is available on request. OpenSpace Field GA February 2026. Novade from Novade Solutions is an AI-powered construction field management platform used by 100,000+ users in 25+ countries. Pricing: Free plan (up to 5 users, 5 projects). 100,000+ users across 25+ countries. Benetics AI from Benetics AI is a voice-activated AI platform for construction field documentation including daily reporting, punch lists, and RFI creation, with real-time translation across 30+ languages. Pricing: Tiered per-user plans (Solo, Team, Enterprise). 250+ construction firms across Europe and North America. BuilderHelp from BuilderHelp is an AI-driven construction management platform targeting $5M-$65M general contractors, launched June 1, 2026. Pricing is available on request. Launched June 1, 2026 with 50+ GC waitlist. KYRO AI from KYRO AI is an unified field management platform for construction, vegetation management, and storm restoration contractors. Pricing: $21/user/month (Basic, billed annually). No independently documented case studies published yet. Klutch AI from Klutch AI is a multi-agent AI construction management platform offering specialized agents across the project lifecycle: Archie AI for zoning and permit review (up to 50% faster permit cycles), Bob AI for capturing punch items, safety forms, and daily jobsite updates via SMS, WhatsApp, and Telegram without requiring a portal login, Petra AI for takeoffs, estimates, bid leveling, and vendor scoring, and Hailey AI for warranty ticket management linked to vendor performance. Pricing is available on request. Saves 10+ hours/week per project manager. Opusense AI from Opusense AI is a Y Combinator-backed (X25 batch) AI-powered field inspection report automation. Pricing is available on request. Claims up to 80% reduction in inspection report production time. Hardline from Hardline is a voice-first construction documentation tool that captures jobsite phone calls and verbal conversations in English and Spanish, transcribes them in real time, and uses AI to extract and structure action items into daily logs, RFIs, punch lists, change orders, tasks, and meeting notes automatically from the phone call itself. Pricing is available on request. Saves superintendents up to 3 hours/day in documentation time. Track3D from Track3D is an AI-powered Reality Intelligence platform that unifies 360 cameras, drones, laser scanners, and LiDAR data into a single platform (VisualTrack) and applies AI-driven construction progress tracking (ProgressTrack). Pricing is available on request. Named BuiltWorlds 2026 Top 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions. Doxel AI from Doxel is an AI-powered construction progress tracking platform that uses 360-degree cameras and computer vision to compare actual work-in-place against BIM models in real time. Pricing is available on request. DPR Construction is among notable customers. NavigateAI from NavigateAI is an AI copilot for field workers in construction and property maintenance, running on smartphones and Meta Glasses to provide real-time hands-free guidance. Pricing is available on request. $25M seed raised at launch (May 26, 2026). Turning to time and materials billing and payment automation. HCSS HeavyJob from HCSS (Heavy Construction Systems Specialists) is an AI-enhanced field operations and cost management platform for heavy civil and specialty contractors. Pricing: $60-$4,000+/user/year depending on modules. Used by thousands of heavy civil contractors across North America. FOUNDATION T&M Billing Module from FOUNDATION Software is a T&M billing and construction accounting platform backed by Thoma Bravo private equity. Pricing is available on request. Produces precise T&M invoices; deep integration with construction accounting eliminates duplicate data entry.. SmartBarrel from SmartBarrel is an AI biometric workforce management system combining facial recognition time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, validating each worker against a saved facial profile even in jobsite conditions.. Pricing is available on request. Eliminates buddy-punching; provides accurate T&M labor documentation; adds PPE compliance monitoring layer to time tracking hardware.. Rhumbix from Rhumbix (acquired by Autodesk, closed March 31, 2026) is a mobile-first workforce tracking and T&M billing platform for construction. Pricing is available on request. 50% reduction in T&M processing time; digital T&M tag processing reduced from 2-6 weeks to 1-2 days; cost overruns caught 2-3 weeks earlier. Siteline from Siteline is a billing and payment management platform built exclusively for commercial subcontractors. Pricing is available on request. Customers save an average of 7 days per week across billing workflows. Adaptive from Adaptive is an AI-native construction accounting platform that continuously captures and codes every cost to the correct job and cost code in real time. Pricing: Starts at $1,000/month (Basic plan, unlimited users, projects, and payments). Best of IBS 2026 Award winner (Business Solution Software category). SubBase from SubBase is an AI-powered materials procurement and invoice reconciliation platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing GCs. Pricing is available on request. 21-second average AI reconciliation vs. Payra from Payra is an AI-enabled accounts receivable automation platform that embeds into existing construction ERPs to enable ACH and card digital payment acceptance, automate cash application, and reconcile payments to invoices in real time. Pricing is available on request. 20% reduction in Days Sales Outstanding, 75% decrease in past-due invoices, significant admin time savings (customer-reported). Intuit Enterprise Suite Construction Edition from Intuit is a purpose-built AI-powered ERP for mid-market contractors launched February 11, 2026. Pricing: Open beta at no charge for existing Intuit Enterprise Suite customers. AI-driven cost and change order tracking available in February 2026 open beta. Trimble Pay from Trimble is a pay application and lien waiver management platform (formerly Flashtract) fully integrated into Trimble Construction One following Trimble's 2024 acquisition. Pricing: Free for subs submitting docs. Seamless integration into Trimble Construction One; free sub access eliminates adoption friction.. Trayd from Trayd is a all-in-one back-office operating system for specialty trade contractors covering payroll, HR, compliance, and labor cost tracking. Pricing is available on request. Reduces weekly payroll processing from 14 hours to 27 minutes — a 31x productivity gain. inBuild from inBuild is an AI-powered invoice processing platform purpose-built for contractors running Procore and QuickBooks. Pricing: Starting at $299/month. Eliminates manual invoice entry between Procore and QuickBooks. Field Materials from Field Materials AI is an AI procurement platform for specialty subcontractors and self-performing general contractors that automates materials purchasing from RFQ through purchase orders, invoices, and delivery receipt matching. Pricing is available on request. Saves 5-10% in material costs and removes 90% of processing time for material purchasing. BlueCollar from BlueCollar (bluecollar.cloud) is an AI-powered construction management ERP built on Oracle NetSuite for specialty trades and GCs. Pricing: $2,000–$8,000/month depending on setup and modules. AI analytics surface real-time financial trends and flag anomalies across jobs. Earlytrade from Earlytrade is an AI-powered subcontractor payments marketplace that gives specialty subs access to early payment on receivables rather than waiting 60-90 days for GC payment. Pricing: Transaction-based. $25M total raised. Werx from Werx is a cloud-based construction management platform built for small to midsized specialty contractors covering T&M billing, AIA-style billing with Schedule of Values and retainage tracking, flexible estimating, job costing, time tracking via mobile field app, and QuickBooks Online sync. Pricing: Starter $9/month. Flexible T&M and AIA billing at entry-level pricing makes it accessible to single-trade specialty subs and small shops that cannot justify enterprise platform costs.. Vergo from Vergo is an AI-powered accounts payable automation platform built specifically for construction finance and accounting teams. Pricing is available on request. Specialty contractors typically reduce invoice processing time 60-80% and eliminate 40+ hours of manual data entry monthly at 500 invoices per month. Kaya AI from Kaya AI is an AI-powered construction supply chain intelligence and procurement automation platform focused on data center and mission-critical infrastructure projects. Pricing is available on request. 80% reduction in procurement management time. Now, change order and document management. Document Crunch from Document Crunch (acquired by Trimble, 2026) is an AI contract review platform acquired by Trimble in 2026. Pricing is being integrated under new ownership following acquisition. Deployed across 10,000+ customer projects processing $350B+ in annual construction volume. Procore Change Order Management with AI Assist from Procore Technologies is an AI-powered change order preparation and management within Procore. Pricing: Included in Procore platform. Speeds change order preparation and approval; reduces financial tracking errors.. Pelles AI from Pelles AI is a generative AI platform that creates RFIs, submittal logs, and proposals in your company's existing format, giving smaller teams the output speed of a much larger document production operation. Pricing is available on request. Maintains consistent formatting across document types; enables small teams to produce proposals and documentation at scale.. Clearstory from Clearstory is a change order management platform with AI Root Cause Analysis, automated classification, and bulk AI import capabilities. Pricing: Free (Basic tier). Suffolk Construction standard across national portfolio. Bluebeam Max from Bluebeam is a global AI intelligence layer on top of Bluebeam Revu, the construction document platform used by millions of AEC professionals. Pricing: $590/user/year introductory pricing through 2027 renewal. Smart Review auto-identifies design issues and scope gaps. Structured AI from Structured AI is an AI drawing QA/QC and compliance checking platform that scans every engineering drawing sheet against building codes, firm standards, fire, accessibility, and coordination requirements simultaneously. Pricing is available on request. Named BuiltWorlds 40 AI-Driven AEC Solutions to Watch in 2026. Buildcheck from Buildcheck is an AI-powered design review platform founded at Stanford University that scans construction drawings across all major disciplines, including architectural, structural, civil, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing, to detect errors, omissions, and coordination conflicts before work begins. Pricing is available on request. 10-35x ROI by preventing costly field issues during pre-construction. Billy from Billy (billyforinsurance.com) is an AI-powered construction insurance compliance and vendor prequalification platform that combines COI tracking and vendor prequalification in one system. Pricing: Self-service: $3–$10/vendor/year. COI review time reduced from 9 minutes to under 1 minute per document. LightTable from LightTable is an AI-native QA/QC and pre-construction intelligence platform that reads and analyzes construction drawings across 35+ scopes at the level of an experienced architect or engineer. Pricing is available on request. 20M+ sq ft of construction documents reviewed. Moving to proposal generation and pre-bid analysis. BidIntell from BidIntell is an AI pre-bid scoring platform that helps specialty subs invest estimating hours only on the invitations that fit their win profile before a single takeoff is started. Pricing: $49/user/month. Reduces wasted estimating hours on low-probability bids; pre-bid layer decision support.. Flowcase from Flowcase is an AI proposal management platform that matches company experience and employee qualifications to RFP requirements and auto-populates bid templates. Pricing is available on request. Matches team experience to RFP requirements; accelerates proposal assembly.. ContraVault from ContraVault is an AI-driven proposal management platform that takes teams from opportunity qualification to submission-ready proposal using AI to assemble, draft, review, and refine content. Pricing is available on request. Average proposal time reduced from 34 hours to 14 hours (60% improvement). ConCntric from ConCntric is an AI preconstruction platform that unifies estimating, planning, and procurement from concept to GMP. Its Amplify agentic AI solution enables preconstruction teams to plan more thoroughly and respond to client needs with a fraction of manual effort. Pricing is available on request. Unifies estimating, planning, and procurement in one platform. Provision from Provision is a Y Combinator-backed preconstruction risk reduction platform. Pricing is available on request. Generates complete scope of work in under 60 minutes vs. MeltPlan from MeltPlan is an AI bid leveling platform that reads subcontractor PDF proposals, maps them against the project scope and RFP, flags exclusions and missing line items, and outputs a normalized comparison matrix showing the true cost basis of each bid. Pricing is available on request. 340% growth in adoption by top 400 GCs from 2024 to 2026. LeanCon from LeanCon is an AI preconstruction planning platform that generates detailed projections covering scope definition, cost estimation, logistics planning, and construction sequencing in approximately 7 minutes — replacing a process that traditionally takes months and costs upward of $2 million per project. Pricing is available on request. $650M+ in live project deployments across New York. Brickanta from Brickanta is a agentic AI platform for construction pre-construction workflows, starting with bid analysis, RFP generation, and cost estimation. Pricing is available on request. Hundreds of users across 11 countries on 4 continents. Next, project scheduling with artificial intelligence. ALICE Technologies from ALICE Technologies is a generative AI scheduling platform for capital projects. Pricing is available on request. McKinsey partnership validates enterprise applicability. Phoenix Project Manager from Phoenix Project Manager is a critical path scheduling platform built for specialty contractors who need resource-loaded schedules without the complexity of Primavera P6. Pricing: $1,800/year. Simplifies resource-loaded scheduling for MEP trades; labor histogram provides portfolio-wide visibility.. Oracle Primavera P6 from Oracle is an industry standard on large complex projects for critical path method (CPM) scheduling, earned value management, and portfolio controls. Pricing: $2,100-$2,500/user/year. Industry standard for large GCs and capital projects; integrations with ALICE for AI scheduling.. nPlan from nPlan is an AI scheduling platform trained on 750,000 historical schedules representing $2T+ in construction spend. Pricing is available on request. 750,000 historical schedules in training data. SmartPM from SmartPM Technologies is an AI-driven automated project controls and schedule analytics platform that integrates with existing CPM scheduling software to flag delays and critical path risks in real time. Pricing: $400/project/month. Real-time delay and critical path risk flagging overlayed on existing schedules. Outbuild from Outbuild is an AI-powered construction execution platform that launched Project Intelligence (April 2026) adding mobile field app for superintendents, AI Assistant for natural language schedule queries, and executive dashboard for portfolio-wide project visibility. Pricing: Scales based on annual construction volume. 400+ customers managing $50B+ in active projects. Planera from Planera is an AI-powered CPM scheduling platform with unlimited-user collaboration and project-based pricing. Pricing: Project-based pricing (volume-dependent). 600% growth in six months. Civalgo from Civalgo is an AI-powered operational scheduling and field management platform built for civil specialty subcontractors and small-to-mid civil construction firms. Pricing is available on request. Links schedule to performance tracking for civil specialty subs; AI forecasts project outcomes for cost, time, and field resources. Mastt from Mastt is an AI-powered construction project management software for project owners, owner's representatives, and GCs. Pricing: Free for first project. AI reduces manual entry, accelerates contract reviews, and flags compliance requirements. And finally, safety and compliance monitoring. Intenseye from Intenseye is an AI-powered construction safety platform using existing camera infrastructure to detect 50+ categories of unsafe acts and conditions in real time. Pricing is available on request. Detects 50+ unsafe acts/conditions in real time. Voxel AI from Voxel AI is an AI-powered safety monitoring platform offering 30-50% lower cost than comparable enterprise platforms. Pricing: Structured around camera count. 30-50% cost savings vs. Oracle Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety from Oracle is an AI-powered safety platform launched March 2026 for large GCs and owner teams running complex projects. Pricing is available on request. Predictive incident forecasting for proactive safety management. Pillar Technologies from Pillar Technologies is a construction site risk management platform using connected environmental sensors alongside visual monitoring to deliver real-time hazard detection. Pricing is available on request. Combines environmental monitoring with visual hazard detection; early warning for concrete curing and atmospheric issues.. DroneDeploy Safety AI from DroneDeploy is an AI hazard detection platform analyzing drone imagery and site camera data against OSHA standards to detect missing guardrails, exposed edges, PPE violations, and other construction hazards. Pricing: $349/month (Individual plan, billed annually). Beta customers: 89% reduction in unsafe conditions within 3 weeks. viAct from viAct is an AI safety platform that transforms existing site cameras into AI safety monitors using 200+ specialized detection modules covering PPE compliance, fall hazards, equipment operation, confined spaces, and traffic management. Pricing: $200/camera/module/month. 200+ specialized detection modules; modular activation reduces cost. Fyld from Fyld is an AI-powered workplace safety platform for construction and infrastructure field teams. Pricing is available on request. 48% reduction in serious workplace incidents (customer-reported). Safety Evolution SE-AI from Safety Evolution is an AI compliance and safety management platform that replaces 20+ hours weekly of administrative safety work with three AI agents covering compliance monitoring, workforce training management, and safety intelligence. Pricing: Tiered per-user pricing. Replaces 20+ hours/week of administrative work. Kwant.AI from Kwant.AI is an AI workforce management and safety platform combining ATEX-certified smart wearables, IoT sensors, and AI analytics to automate safety orientation, track certifications, and detect incidents in real time. Pricing: Starting ~$50,000 for single-site rollout (wearables + sensors). ATEX-certified wearables for explosive atmospheres. Hakimo AI from Hakimo AI is an AI-powered physical security and safety monitoring platform for jobsites. Pricing is available on request. 90% reduction in incidents (customer-reported). Sensera Systems from Sensera Systems is an AI-powered jobsite intelligence platform combining solar-powered, cellular-connected cameras with AI and machine learning via its SiteCloud software. Pricing is available on request. $27M Series B (February 2026). FieldFlō from FieldFlō is a project and safety management platform built specifically for specialty subcontractors in demolition, asbestos abatement, concrete, masonry, and environmental remediation. Pricing is available on request. $35M growth investment from Mainsail Partners (October 2025). Newmetrix from Newmetrix (formerly Smartvid.io) is a computer vision AI safety monitoring platform that analyzes jobsite photos and videos to automatically detect safety hazards and predict project risk. Pricing is available on request. 100+ safety hazard categories detected automatically from photos and videos. Field1st from Field1st is an AI-powered safety management platform purpose-built for high-risk industries including construction, utilities, energy, and oil and gas. Pricing is available on request. Enterprises reporting improved proactive risk management versus reactive incident tracking. Nyfty.ai from Nyfty.ai is a agentic AI safety and field operations automation platform for construction that automates safety compliance workflows triggered by SMS link and completed in a browser-based chat interface without requiring a separate portal login. Pricing is available on request. Turner Construction named Nyfty an approved safety automation platform. Now for the broader market picture. On what owners and large general contractors are expecting from specialty subs: Major general contractors are increasingly requiring specialty subcontractors to actively manage submittals, RFIs, and daily documentation inside Procore or Autodesk Build as a condition of project participation. BIM coordination and model-based submittals are expected on commercial projects above ten million dollars. Digital daily reports with photo documentation and real-time cost tracking are moving from nice-to-have to contractual obligation. Subs who cannot interface digitally with GC platforms are being filtered from bid lists before the first conversation happens. AI adoption in construction has now doubled year over year, with thirty-eight percent of contractors reporting measurable business impact from AI — up from seventeen percent just one year ago — and ninety-four percent of those using AI plan to increase their usage in 2026. Owners and large GCs are beginning to require documented AI-driven risk management, particularly for change orders and safety compliance on projects above twenty-five million dollars. On realistic adoption cost for a specialty sub with five to twenty project managers: On realistic adoption cost for a specialty sub with five to twenty project managers: a functional AI stack covering estimating, field documentation, submittal management, and billing automation typically runs between fifteen thousand and eighty thousand dollars per year in software costs. Training represents an additional fifteen to twenty percent of the total investment and is the factor most consistently underestimated. Implementation typically takes sixty to ninety days before teams reach operational efficiency. Most teams recover their AI investment within three to six months. And on documented return on investment: A 2025-2026 industry report found that sixty-eight percent of AI early adopters in construction have saved at least fifty thousand dollars, and forty-six percent have recovered between five hundred and one thousand hours of labor. The average return across the industry is three and a half times the investment within eighteen months, with top performers reporting ten times ROI. Bid preparation time is down by as much as sixty percent, and contractors using AI tools can respond to two to three times more RFQs with the same staff. Subcontractors on Siteline have reduced their Days Sales Outstanding to an average of fifty-three days versus the industry average of ninety-six days — a forty-four percent improvement. Trunk Tools customers have documented over sixty thousand dollars saved on four-project pilots. Dynamic pricing AI for subcontractors has saved eight to twelve percent on labor expenses. That wraps your weekly AI tools briefing. June 15th, 2026. Stay sharp, bid smart, and build safe.

market_context.owner_and_gc_expectations: Major general contractors are increasingly requiring specialty subcontractors to actively manage submittals, RFIs, and daily documentation inside Procore or Autodesk Build as a condition of project participation. BIM coordination and model-based submittals are expected on commercial projects above ten million dollars. Digital daily reports with photo documentation and real-time cost tracking are moving from nice-to-have to contractual obligation. Subs who cannot interface digitally with GC platforms are being filtered from bid lists before the first conversation happens. AI adoption in construction has now doubled year over year, with thirty-eight percent of contractors reporting measurable business impact from AI — up from seventeen percent just one year ago — and ninety-four percent of those using AI plan to increase their usage in 2026. Owners and large GCs are beginning to require documented AI-driven risk management, particularly for change orders and safety compliance on projects above twenty-five million dollars. The AGC 2026 outlook survey found 61% of construction firms are currently using AI or actively planning to increase AI investment, up from 38% who already report measurable business impact — up from 17% one year ago. AI contract risk management provisions are increasingly flowing down through prime contracts into subcontract language, making tool adoption a contractual consideration on projects above $25M.
market_context.realistic_adoption_cost: On realistic adoption cost for a specialty sub with five to twenty project managers: a functional AI stack covering estimating, field documentation, submittal management, and billing automation typically runs between fifteen thousand and eighty thousand dollars per year in software costs. Training represents an additional fifteen to twenty percent of the total investment and is the factor most consistently underestimated. Implementation typically takes sixty to ninety days before teams reach operational efficiency. Most teams recover their AI investment within three to six months.
market_context.documented_roi: A 2025-2026 industry report found that sixty-eight percent of AI early adopters in construction have saved at least fifty thousand dollars, and forty-six percent have recovered between five hundred and one thousand hours of labor. The average return across the industry is three and a half times the investment within eighteen months, with top performers reporting ten times ROI. Bid preparation time is down by as much as sixty percent, and contractors using AI tools can respond to two to three times more RFQs with the same staff. Subcontractors on Siteline have reduced their Days Sales Outstanding to an average of fifty-three days versus the industry average of ninety-six days — a forty-four percent improvement. Trunk Tools customers have documented over sixty thousand dollars saved on four-project pilots. Dynamic pricing AI for subcontractors has saved eight to twelve percent on labor expenses.
